# Master Service Agreement (MSA)

---

## Document Control

| Field              | Value                                       |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| **MSA Number**     | MSA-[YYYY]-[NNNN]                           |
| **Effective Date** | [DD-MMM-YYYY]                               |
| **Customer**       | [Customer Legal Entity Name] ("Customer")   |
| **Vendor**         | [Vendor Legal Entity Name] ("Vendor")       |
| **Initial Term**   | [X] years from Effective Date               |
| **Governing Law**  | State of [State], [Country]                 |
| **Classification** | Confidential --- Attorney-Client Privileged |

### Revision History

| Rev | Date   | Author | Description             |
| --- | ------ | ------ | ----------------------- |
| 1.0 | [Date] | [Name] | Initial agreement       |
| 1.1 | [Date] | [Name] | [Amendment description] |

---

## Recitals

WHEREAS, Customer desires to engage Vendor to provide certain professional services; and

WHEREAS, Vendor has the expertise, personnel, and resources to perform such services; and

WHEREAS, the parties desire to establish the general terms and conditions under which Vendor will provide services to Customer pursuant to individual Statements of Work;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

---

## Article 1 --- Definitions

1.1 **"Agreement"** means this Master Service Agreement, including all exhibits, schedules, and Statements of Work executed hereunder.

1.2 **"Confidential Information"** means all non-public information disclosed by either party, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.

1.3 **"Deliverables"** means all work product, reports, documentation, and materials created by Vendor in performance of Services under a SOW.

1.4 **"Intellectual Property"** means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.

1.5 **"Services"** means the professional services described in each Statement of Work executed under this Agreement.

1.6 **"Statement of Work" or "SOW"** means a written document executed by both parties that describes the specific Services to be performed, deliverables, timeline, and fees.

1.7 **"Term"** has the meaning set forth in Article 4.

---

## Article 2 --- Services

2.1 **Engagement.** Vendor shall provide Services as described in individually executed SOWs. Each SOW shall reference this Agreement and be governed by its terms.

2.2 **Standard of Performance.** Vendor shall perform all Services in a professional and workmanlike manner, consistent with generally accepted industry standards and practices.

2.3 **Personnel.** Vendor shall assign qualified personnel to perform Services. Vendor shall not replace key personnel identified in a SOW without [X] business days prior written notice and Customer's reasonable approval.

2.4 **Subcontractors.** Vendor shall not subcontract any portion of the Services without Customer's prior written consent. Vendor remains fully responsible for the performance of any approved subcontractors.

2.5 **Compliance.** Vendor shall comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, regulations, and ordinances in performing Services.

---

## Article 3 --- Compensation and Payment

### 3.1 Fees

| Term            | Details                                              |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| Rate Structure  | As specified in each SOW                             |
| Rate Escalation | Not to exceed [X]% annually                          |
| Expenses        | Pre-approved only; subject to Customer travel policy |
| Invoicing       | [Monthly / Upon milestone completion]                |
| Payment Terms   | Net [30/45/60] days from receipt of approved invoice |
| Late Payment    | [X]% monthly on undisputed overdue amounts           |
| Currency        | [USD / Local currency]                               |

### 3.2 Invoicing Requirements

All invoices shall include:

- SOW number and reference
- Detailed description of Services performed
- Time period covered
- Hours worked by individual (for T&M engagements)
- Milestone achieved (for fixed-price engagements)
- Itemized expenses with receipts
- Applicable taxes

### 3.3 Disputed Invoices

Customer may dispute any invoice or portion thereof in good faith within [X] days of receipt. Customer shall pay undisputed amounts by the due date. Parties shall use commercially reasonable efforts to resolve disputes within [X] business days.

### 3.4 Audit Rights

Customer may audit Vendor's books and records related to this Agreement upon [X] days written notice, no more than once per calendar year. Vendor shall maintain records for [X] years following the applicable SOW completion.

---

## Article 4 --- Term and Renewal

4.1 **Initial Term.** This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for [X] years ("Initial Term").

4.2 **Renewal.** Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive [X]-year periods ("Renewal Terms") unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least [X] days prior to expiration.

4.3 **SOW Survival.** Active SOWs shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement until completed or separately terminated.

---

## Article 5 --- Termination

| Termination Type            | Notice Period                         | Conditions                                                  | Financial Obligation                                                                 |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| For Convenience             | [X] days written notice               | No cause required                                           | Payment for Services rendered through termination date plus approved wind-down costs |
| For Cause (Material Breach) | [X] days to cure after written notice | Uncured material breach                                     | Payment for conforming Services rendered; non-breaching party may seek damages       |
| For Cause (Insolvency)      | Immediate                             | Bankruptcy, insolvency, assignment for benefit of creditors | Payment for Services rendered through termination date                               |
| Non-Renewal                 | [X] days prior to expiration          | Written notice of non-renewal                               | Payment through end of current term                                                  |
| Regulatory / Force Majeure  | [X] days                              | Change in law or extended force majeure (>[X] days)         | Payment for Services rendered; no penalties                                          |

5.2 **Effect of Termination.** Upon termination or expiration:

1. Vendor shall cease all Services and incur no further charges
2. Vendor shall deliver all completed and in-progress Deliverables
3. Vendor shall provide reasonable transition assistance for up to [X] days
4. Customer shall pay all undisputed amounts for Services rendered
5. Sections surviving termination: Articles 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13

---

## Article 6 --- Confidentiality

6.1 **Obligations.** Each party shall: (a) hold Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose it to third parties without prior written consent; (c) use it only for purposes of this Agreement; (d) protect it with no less than reasonable care.

6.2 **Exclusions.** Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party; (b) was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of Confidential Information; (d) is rightfully obtained from a third party without restriction.

6.3 **Compelled Disclosure.** If required to disclose by law, regulation, or court order, the receiving party shall provide prompt written notice and cooperate to obtain protective treatment.

6.4 **Return of Materials.** Upon termination or request, each party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information and certify destruction in writing within [X] business days.

6.5 **Duration.** Confidentiality obligations survive termination for [X] years. Trade secrets are protected indefinitely.

---

## Article 7 --- Intellectual Property

7.1 **Pre-Existing IP.** Each party retains all rights in its pre-existing Intellectual Property. Neither party grants the other any rights except as expressly stated herein.

7.2 **Work Product.** [ ] Select one:

- [ ] **Customer Owns:** All Deliverables and work product are works made for hire and shall be owned exclusively by Customer.
- [ ] **Vendor Owns with License:** Vendor retains ownership; Customer receives a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, modify, and sublicense Deliverables.
- [ ] **Joint Ownership:** Deliverables are jointly owned; each party may use without accounting to the other.

  7.3 **Vendor Tools.** Vendor retains ownership of pre-existing tools, methodologies, and frameworks. Customer receives a non-exclusive license to use such tools solely as embedded in Deliverables.

  7.4 **Customer Data.** Customer retains all rights in its data. Vendor shall not use Customer data except as necessary to perform Services.

---

## Article 8 --- Representations and Warranties

8.1 **Mutual Warranties.** Each party represents and warrants that: (a) it has authority to enter this Agreement; (b) execution does not violate other agreements; (c) it will comply with applicable laws.

8.2 **Vendor Warranties.** Vendor represents and warrants that:

1. Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner
2. Deliverables shall conform to specifications in the applicable SOW
3. Deliverables shall not infringe any third-party Intellectual Property rights
4. Vendor personnel are properly qualified and authorized to work
5. Vendor maintains all required licenses and certifications

8.3 **Warranty Period.** Vendor shall correct, at no additional cost, any Services or Deliverables that fail to conform to warranties for [X] months following acceptance.

8.4 **Disclaimer.** EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

---

## Article 9 --- Indemnification

9.1 **Vendor Indemnification.** Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Customer from claims arising from: (a) Vendor's negligence or willful misconduct; (b) infringement of third-party IP rights; (c) Vendor's breach of this Agreement; (d) injury to persons or damage to property caused by Vendor personnel.

9.2 **Customer Indemnification.** Customer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor from claims arising from: (a) Customer's negligence or willful misconduct; (b) Customer-provided materials that infringe third-party rights; (c) Customer's breach of this Agreement.

9.3 **Procedure.** The indemnified party shall: (a) promptly notify the indemnifying party; (b) grant sole control of defense and settlement; (c) provide reasonable cooperation at the indemnifying party's expense.

---

## Article 10 --- Limitation of Liability

10.1 **Direct Damages Cap.** EACH PARTY'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF: (a) $[X]; OR (b) FEES PAID OR PAYABLE UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE [X]-MONTH PERIOD PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

10.2 **Exclusion of Consequential Damages.** IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, OR LOSS OF DATA.

10.3 **Exceptions.** The limitations in 10.1 and 10.2 shall not apply to: (a) breaches of confidentiality; (b) indemnification obligations; (c) willful misconduct or gross negligence; (d) infringement of Intellectual Property rights.

---

## Article 11 --- Insurance

Vendor shall maintain the following insurance coverage throughout the Term:

| Coverage Type                | Minimum Amount                         | Additional Requirements                  |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Commercial General Liability | $[X]M per occurrence / $[X]M aggregate | Customer named as additional insured     |
| Professional Liability (E&O) | $[X]M per claim / $[X]M aggregate      | Retroactive date prior to Effective Date |
| Workers' Compensation        | Statutory limits                       | As required by applicable law            |
| Employer's Liability         | $[X]M per accident                     |                                          |
| Cyber Liability              | $[X]M per claim                        | Data breach response coverage            |
| Automobile Liability         | $[X]M combined single limit            | If applicable                            |
| Umbrella / Excess            | $[X]M                                  | Above primary policies                   |

Vendor shall provide certificates of insurance upon request and [X] days prior written notice of cancellation or material change.

---

## Article 12 --- Data Protection and Security

12.1 **Data Handling.** Vendor shall process Customer data only as necessary to perform Services and in accordance with Customer's data classification policies.

12.2 **Security Standards.** Vendor shall maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards consistent with [SOC 2 Type II / ISO 27001 / NIST 800-53] requirements.

12.3 **Breach Notification.** Vendor shall notify Customer of any security incident involving Customer data within [X] hours of discovery.

12.4 **Data Return/Destruction.** Upon termination, Vendor shall return or securely destroy all Customer data within [X] business days and provide written certification.

---

## Article 13 --- Dispute Resolution

13.1 **Escalation.** Disputes shall first be escalated to senior management of each party for resolution within [X] business days.

13.2 **Mediation.** If unresolved, parties shall submit to mediation administered by [Mediation body] within [X] days.

13.3 **Binding Resolution.** If mediation fails:

- [ ] **Arbitration:** Binding arbitration under [AAA/JAMS] rules; [City, State]; [X] arbitrator(s)
- [ ] **Litigation:** Exclusive jurisdiction of courts in [County, State]

  13.4 **Injunctive Relief.** Nothing herein prevents either party from seeking injunctive or equitable relief to protect Intellectual Property or Confidential Information.

  13.5 **Prevailing Party.** The prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.

---

## Article 14 --- General Provisions

14.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Agreement, including all SOWs and exhibits, constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes all prior negotiations and agreements.

14.2 **Amendments.** Modifications must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

14.3 **Assignment.** Neither party may assign this Agreement without prior written consent, except in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets.

14.4 **Force Majeure.** Neither party shall be liable for delays caused by events beyond reasonable control, provided prompt notice and reasonable mitigation efforts.

14.5 **Notices.** All notices shall be in writing and delivered to the addresses set forth below, effective upon receipt.

14.6 **Severability.** If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect.

14.7 **Waiver.** Failure to enforce any provision shall not constitute a waiver of future enforcement.

14.8 **Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original.

---
